subtitle: Lateral ligament sprain — most common ankle injury

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Lateral sprains most common on trail runs and fatigued late-run states

mild.description: Mild outer ankle soreness after a roll or uneven surface
mild.can_run_note: Yes on flat roads — avoid trails and uneven surfaces
why: Anterior talofibular ligament (ATFL) sprain from ankle inversion
Ankle instability from old sprains reducing proprioception
Peroneal muscle fatigue reducing ankle protection late in runs
relief: RICE for 24 hours: rest, ice 15 min every 2 hours, compression, elevation
Gentle ankle circles once acute pain settles — day 2 or 3
Ankle alphabet — trace letters with your toe to restore range
prevention: Single-leg balance work daily — progress to unstable surface
Peroneal strengthening: resisted eversion with band
Proprioception training: wobble board or BOSU
Trail shoes with ankle support on technical terrain

moderate.description: Swelling on outer ankle, painful to walk on immediately after
moderate.can_run_note: No running until pain-free walking — typically 5–10 days
why: Grade 2 ATFL sprain with partial ligament tearing
Peroneal tendon injury alongside the sprain
relief: RICE strictly for 48 hours — elevate above heart level
Ankle brace or strapping for support during walking
Physiotherapy for hands-on treatment and rehabilitation
prevention: Full ligament rehabilitation before return to running
Proprioception training is critical — do not skip this
Brace support for first 4–6 weeks of return to running

severe.description: Significant swelling, unable to weight-bear, severe pain
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible fracture must be ruled out
why: Possible avulsion fracture of the fibula — Ottawa rules apply
relief: Ottawa rules: if tender on the posterior fibula or cannot take 4 steps — get an X-ray
Immobilisation boot if fracture ruled out but severe instability
prevention: Full 8–12 week rehabilitation programme before return to sport
Long-term proprioception programme to prevent recurrence

see_physio_if: Unable to take 4 steps immediately after the injury
Tenderness directly on the bony prominences
Swelling that does not reduce after 48 hours of RICE
